records.description: This change involves Application Server Decommission in the DEV environment as part of infrastructure lifecycle management.
records.u_reason_for_change: Routine maintenance, compliance requirement, or security hardening.
records.implementation_plan:
1. Obtain stakeholder approval.
2. Validate no active dependencies.
3. Remove from load balancer.
4. Disable monitoring.
5. Take final backup.
6. Shutdown VM.
7. Remove DNS and update CMDB.
records.test_plan: Confirm no traffic routed before shutdown.
records.backout_plan: Restore VM snapshot and re-add to load balancer.
records.justification: Aligned with operational governance and infrastructure reliability standards.

records.description: This change involves Oracle PSU Patch Update in the DR environment as part of infrastructure lifecycle management.
records.u_reason_for_change: Routine maintenance, compliance requirement, or security hardening.
records.implementation_plan:
1. Take RMAN full backup.
2. Stop database services.
3. Apply PSU patch using OPatch.
4. Restart database.
5. Validate alert.log.
6. Execute health check queries.
records.risk_impact_analysis: Patch failure may prevent DB startup.
records.test_plan: Confirm DB open mode and run validation queries.
records.justification: Aligned with operational governance and infrastructure reliability standards.

records.description: This change involves Linux Kernel Security Update in the STAGE environment as part of infrastructure lifecycle management.
records.u_reason_for_change: Routine maintenance, compliance requirement, or security hardening.
records.implementation_plan:
1. Check current kernel version using uname -r.
2. Run yum update --security.
3. Reboot system.
4. Validate services using systemctl status.
5. Check logs via journalctl.
records.test_plan: Confirm system services are active post reboot.
records.backout_plan: Restore VM snapshot if instability detected.
records.justification: Aligned with operational governance and infrastructure reliability standards.

records.description: This change involves Kubernetes Deployment Rolling Restart in the DR environment as part of infrastructure lifecycle management.
records.u_reason_for_change: Routine maintenance, compliance requirement, or security hardening.
records.implementation_plan:
1. Validate cluster health using kubectl get nodes.
2. Verify pod status using kubectl get pods -A.
3. Execute kubectl rollout restart deployment <deployment-name>.
4. Monitor rollout using kubectl rollout status deployment.
5. Validate readiness probes.
6. Check logs using kubectl logs.
7. Confirm no crashloop events.
records.risk_impact_analysis: Temporary pod unavailability during rolling restart.
records.test_plan: Ensure all pods reach Running state and services return HTTP 200.
records.justification: Aligned with operational governance and infrastructure reliability standards.
